notes: new name. Abram's name was changed to Abraham, Jacob's to Israel, and Saul's to Paul-all to signify a new character, a new devotion, a new condition, a new life. So it will be with Israel. The new name given to Israel may be The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (Mc Conkie, Millennial Messiah, 131).
The JST replaces "Hephzibah" with "Delightful," and "Beulah" with "Union."
The King James clause "so shall thy sons marry thee" is changed in the JST to "so shall thy God marry
Watchmen [prophets] until the Lord's kingdom is established.
no more give thy corn . . . for thine enemies . . . they that have gathered it shall eat it. This statement is a reversal of the cursing Israel was threatened with in Deuteronomy:

Isaiah has pictured this great day when the Lord shall come with his garments, or apparel, red and glorious, to take vengeance on the ungodly. [Isaiah 63:1-6.] This will be a day of mourning to the wicked, but a day of gladness to all who have kept his commandments. Do not let any one think that this is merely figurative language, it is literal, and as surely as we live that day of wrath will come when the
